memoryArtifacts.headline: Life Sketch of Richard Pysing
memoryArtifacts.description: Richard Pysing was born about 1525.  The family was of "East Myng and Crewkerne".  He was the son of John Peyssun of Abbotsbury.  The name is sometimes written as Peyssum, Peson, and Pysing.  Richa …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Alexander Pysing於1521年出生在英國多瑟特阿波次波利時，他的父親John Pysing31歲，母親Elizabeth Salmon27歲。他於1549年與Margery Ann Evered在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1588年6月25日在英國薩默塞特可福可納享年67歲，埋葬在英國多瑟特阿波次波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Alexander Pysing于1521年出生在英国多瑟特阿波次波利时，他的父亲John Pysing31岁，母亲Elizabeth Salmon27岁。他于1549年与Margery Ann Evered在英国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1588年6月25日在英国薩默塞特可福可納享年67岁，埋葬在英国多瑟特阿波次波利。

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Alexander Pysing was born in 1521, in Abbotsbury, Dorset, England, his father, John Pysing, was 31 and his mother, Elizabeth Salmon, was 27. He married Margery Ann Evered in 1549, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 25 June 1588, in Crewkerne, Somerset, England, at the age of 67, and was buried in Abbotsbury, Dorset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Alexander Pysing est né en 1521, à Abbotsbury, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, John Pysing, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Salmon, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Margery Ann Evered en 1549, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 25 juin 1588, à Crewkerne, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Abbotsbury, Dorset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Alexander Pysing 1521 in Abbotsbury, Dorset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Pysing 31 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Salmon 27. Er hat 1549 in England Margery Ann Evered geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 25. Juni 1588 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Crewkerne, Somerset, England und wurde in Abbotsbury, Dorset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Alexander Pysing è nato il nel 1521 nel Abbotsbury, Dorset, England, il suo padre, John Pysing, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Salmon, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Margery Ann Evered nel 1549, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 25 giugno 1588, nel Crewkerne, Somerset, England, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Abbotsbury, Dorset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Alexander Pysingが1521年にイギリスドールセトアボトスブリーで生まれた時，父John Pysingは31歳，母Elizabeth Salmonは27歳であった。1549年にイギリスでMargery Ann Everedと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1588年6月25日にイギリスソメルセトクレウケルンで67歳で死去し，イギリスドールセトアボトスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Alexander Pysing(이)가 1521년 영국 도르세트 애벗스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Pysing(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Salmon(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1549년 영국에서 Margery Ann Evered(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1588년 6월 25일 영국 서머세트 크레w커니에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 도르세트 애벗스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Alexander Pysing nasceu em 1521, em Abbotsbury, Dorset, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Pysing, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Salmon, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Margery Ann Evered em 1549, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 25 de junho de 1588, em Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Abbotsbury, Dorset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Alexander Pysing родился в 1521 г. в местности эббоцбури, дорсет, Англия, его отцу по имени John Pysing было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Salmon, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery Ann Evered в 1549 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 25 июня 1588 г. в местности крюкирн, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности эббоцбури, дорсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Alexander Pysing nació en 1521, en Abbotsbury, Dorset, Inglaterra, su papá, John Pysing, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Salmon, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Margery Ann Evered en 1549, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 25 de junio de 1588, en Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Abbotsbury, Dorset, Inglaterra.
